title: Early education and childcare services proprietors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate academic or nonacademic activities of preschools or childcare centers and programs, including before- and after-school care. Advise and assist students and provide educational and vocational guidance services. Teach students on a short-term basis as a temporary replacement for a regular classroom teacher, typically using the regular teacher's lesson plan. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with parents and staff to discuss educational activities and policies as well as students' behavioral or learning problems.
Determine the scope of educational program offerings and prepare drafts of program schedules and descriptions to estimate staffing and facility requirements.
Prepare and maintain attendance, activity, planning, accounting, or personnel reports and records for officials and agencies, or direct preparation and maintenance activities.
Organize and direct committees of specialists, volunteers, and staff to provide technical and advisory assistance for programs.
Collect and analyze survey data, regulatory information, and demographic and employment trends to forecast enrollment patterns and the need for curriculum changes.
Write articles, manuals, and other publications and assist in the distribution of promotional literature about programs and facilities.
Prepare and submit budget requests or grant proposals to solicit program funding.
Teach classes or courses or provide direct care to children, using lesson plans designed by regular teachers when necessary.
Review and evaluate new and current programs to determine their efficiency, effectiveness, and compliance with regulations, and recommend necessary modifications.
Inform businesses, community groups, and governmental agencies about educational needs, available programs, and program policies.
Review and interpret government codes, and develop procedures to meet these codes, ensuring facility safety, security, and maintenance.
Direct and coordinate activities of teachers or administrators at daycare centers, schools, public agencies, or institutions.
Recruit, hire, train, and evaluate staff, and recommend personnel actions for programs and services.
Determine allocations of funds for staff, supplies, materials, and equipment, and authorize purchases.
Monitor students' progress and assist students and teachers in resolving problems related to learning.
Set educational standards and goals, and help establish policies, procedures, and programs to achieve them.
Plan, direct, and monitor instructional methods and content of educational, vocational, or student activity programs.
Assess students' progress throughout tutoring sessions and provide necessary support.
Assist students with boarding or exiting school buses (irrelevant).
Answer students' questions to support their learning.
Provide private instruction to individual or small groups of students to improve academic performance or prepare for tests.
Teach students study skills, note-taking skills, and test-taking strategies to promote academic success.
Administer, proctor, or score academic assessments (irrelevant).
Develop teaching or training materials, such as handouts, study materials, or quizzes to support instruction.
Collaborate with students, parents, teachers, and school administrators to assess student needs and develop tutoring plans.
Communicate students' progress to students, parents, or teachers through written and verbal methods.
Identify, develop, or implement intervention strategies or individualized education plans (IEPs) for students.
Maintain records of students' assessment results, progress, and feedback while ensuring confidentiality.
Counsel students with adjustment or academic problems to support their educational journey.
Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, or workshops to improve professional competence in education and care.
Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices or supportive technology (irrelevant).
Distribute or collect tests or homework assignments as part of the educational process.
Distribute teaching materials to students to facilitate learning.
Enforce school and class rules to maintain order in the classroom environment.
Operate equipment such as computers or audio-visual aids to enhance presentations.
Provide feedback to students using positive reinforcement techniques to encourage and motivate them.
Supervise students during activities outside the classroom, such as recess and field trips.
Take class attendance and maintain accurate attendance records for students.
Teach a variety of subjects, such as English, mathematics, and social studies, to enrich student learning.
Teach social skills to students to aid in their communication and interpersonal relationships.
Tutor or assist students individually or in small groups to enhance their understanding of subjects.
Prepare lesson plans or learning modules tailored to students' needs and goals for tutoring sessions.
Monitor student performance in academic environments, such as classrooms or laboratories, to offer support.
Review class material with students by discussing text and working through assignments.
Participate in training and development sessions to enhance tutoring practices and learn new techniques.
Prepare and facilitate tutoring workshops or academic support sessions for small groups of students.
Research or recommend textbooks or other learning materials to complement tutoring (irrelevant).
Schedule tutoring appointments with students or their parents to organize educational support sessions.
Travel to students' homes or schools to conduct tutoring sessions (irrelevant).
Organize the tutoring environment to promote productivity and effective learning.
Plan and conduct orientation programs and group conferences to aid individuals in adjusting to new experiences.
Review transcripts to ensure students meet graduation or college entrance requirements, and write letters of recommendation as needed.
Provide information for teachers and staff involved in supporting students in identifying and pursuing employment opportunities.
Compile and study occupational, educational, and economic information to assist counselees with vocational goals (irrelevant).
Address community groups and staff members to explain available counseling services for educational support.
Instruct individuals in career development techniques such as job search and resume writing.
Assess needs for additional assistance and refer clients to appropriate services as needed.
Teach classes and conduct information sessions related to education and career planning.
Prepare reports on students and activities as required by administration to maintain accountability.
Conduct follow-up interviews with counselees to determine if their needs have been met (irrelevant).
Provide special services such as conflict resolution programs that promote positive behaviors among students (irrelevant).
Provide students with information on topics such as college programs and financial aid to aid their educational pursuits.
Encourage students to explore learning opportunities and persevere with challenging tasks to prepare for future experiences.
Provide crisis intervention to students in difficult situations occurring at schools.
Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws and policies.
Counsel individuals or groups to help them address personal, social, or behavioral problems affecting their education.
Refer students to degree programs based on their interests and educational assessments to guide their academic paths.
Supervise, train, and direct professional staff and interns (irrelevant).
Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices and support in accessing facilities (irrelevant).
Counsel students regarding educational issues such as course selection and academic planning.
Sponsor extracurricular activities, such as clubs and academic contests, to engage students outside the classroom (irrelevant).
Refer qualified counselees to employers or services for job placement (irrelevant).
Establish and supervise peer-counseling and peer-tutoring programs to support student learning (irrelevant).
Collaborate with teachers and administrators in evaluating and revising school programs and preparing schedules for curriculum offerings.
Plan, direct, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities to attract students.
Refer students to outside counseling services if needed at any point in their educational journey.
